Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Interface ISlots

Namespace
Pandowdy.EmuCore.Interfaces
Assembly
Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll

Manages Apple IIe expansion slots and coordinates peripheral card I/O and ROM access.

public interface ISlots : IMemory, IConfigurable
Inherited Members
IMemory.Size
IMemory.Read(ushort)
IMemory.Write(ushort, byte)
IMemory.this[ushort]
IConfigurable.GetMetadata()
IConfigurable.ApplyMetadata(string)

Remarks

The ISlots interface coordinates all expansion card operations in the Apple IIe emulator, managing the complex address decoding logic that determines which device (card or system ROM) responds to memory accesses in the $C000-$CFFF range.

Address Space Management:

Address RangePurpose
$C090-$C0FFCard I/O space (16 bytes per slot: $C0n0-$C0nF where n = slot + 8)
$C100-$C7FFCard ROM space (256 bytes per slot: $Cn00-$CnFF where n = slot)
$C800-$CFFFExtended ROM space (2KB shared via bank switching)

Soft Switch Control:
The slots system respects three soft switches that control ROM visibility:

  • INTCXROM ($C006/$C007): Master override—when enabled, internal ROM appears for entire $C100-$CFFF range, disabling all cards
  • SLOTCXROM ($C00A/$C00B): When INTCXROM is off, controls whether slots 1,2,4-7 use card ROM or system ROM
  • SLOTC3ROM ($C00C/$C00D): Independent control for slot 3 (typically 80-column firmware)

Extended ROM Bank Switching:
The $C800-$CFFF region is shared among all slots via the BankSelect mechanism:

  • Accessing a slot's ROM ($Cn00-$CnFF) activates that slot's extended ROM at $C800-$CFFF
  • Accessing $CFFF from any slot disables extended ROM (sets BankSelect to 0)
  • Only one slot's extended ROM can be active at a time

Null Card Behavior:
Empty slots are filled with GetNullCard() instances that return null for all operations, causing the slots system to return floating bus values for reads and no-op for writes.

Configuration Management:
The slots system implements IConfigurable, enabling complete save/restore of the entire peripheral configuration. The metadata includes which cards are installed in which slots, along with each card's configuration (hierarchically embedded). This allows the entire emulator peripheral setup to be persisted, shared, and restored, including disk images, serial settings, and other card-specific state. The metadata format is typically JSON. See IConfigurable for the configuration protocol.

Properties

BankSelect

Gets or sets the currently active slot for extended ROM at $C800-$CFFF.

byte BankSelect { get; set; }

Property Value

byte

A value from 0-7 where:

  • 0 = No slot active (extended ROM disabled)
  • 1-7 = That slot's extended ROM is active at $C800-$CFFF

Remarks

This property implements the Apple IIe's extended ROM bank switching mechanism. The hardware allows only one slot's extended ROM to be visible at $C800-$CFFF at any given time. The active slot is determined by:

  1. Reading/writing $Cn00-$CnFF sets BankSelect to slot n
  2. Reading/writing $CFFF sets BankSelect to 0 (disabled)

The bank selection state is preserved even when INTCXROM is enabled (which overrides extended ROM with system ROM but doesn't clear the selection). This allows the previously selected card's extended ROM to reappear when INTCXROM is disabled.

Example: A Disk II controller in slot 6 has boot ROM at $C600-$C6FF and extended driver code at its extended ROM. When the system reads $C600, slot 6's extended ROM becomes active at $C800-$CFFF. Reading $CFFF disables extended ROM.

Methods

GetCardIn(SlotNumber)

Retrieves the card currently installed in the specified slot.

ICard GetCardIn(SlotNumber slot)

Parameters

slot SlotNumber

The slot number (1-7) to query.

Returns

ICard

The ICard instance installed in the slot. Returns a GetNullCard() instance if the slot is empty.

Remarks

This method provides direct access to the card instance for inspection, debugging, or advanced operations. Normal emulator operation doesn't require calling this method since Read(ushort) and Write(ushort, byte) automatically route to the appropriate card based on address.

Usage Examples: 

// Check what's installed in slot 6
ICard card = slots.GetCardIn(SlotNumber.Slot6);
Console.WriteLine($"Slot 6: {card.Name}");

// Verify a slot is empty
if (slots.GetCardIn(SlotNumber.Slot3).Id == 0) // NullCard.Id == 0
{
    Console.WriteLine("Slot 3 is empty");
}

InstallCard(int, SlotNumber)

Installs a peripheral card in the specified slot using its numeric card ID.

void InstallCard(int id, SlotNumber slot)

Parameters

id int

The unique numeric identifier of the card type to install (see Id).

slot SlotNumber

The slot number (1-7) where the card should be installed.

Remarks

This method uses the ICardFactory to create a new instance of the specified card type via GetCardWithId(int). The card is cloned from a registered prototype, ensuring each slot gets an independent instance.

If a card is already installed in the specified slot, it is replaced by the new card. No state from the previous card is preserved.

Common card IDs:

  • 0 = NullCard (empty slot)
  • 1 = Disk II Controller
  • 2 = Super Serial Card

Exceptions

InvalidOperationException

Thrown if the card factory cannot create a card with the specified ID.

See Also
InstallCard(string, SlotNumber)
RemoveCard(SlotNumber)

InstallCard(string, SlotNumber)

Installs a peripheral card in the specified slot using its human-readable name.

void InstallCard(string name, SlotNumber slot)

Parameters

name string

The name of the card type to install (see Name).

slot SlotNumber

The slot number (1-7) where the card should be installed.

Remarks

This method provides a more user-friendly alternative to InstallCard(int, SlotNumber) by accepting the card's display name instead of its numeric ID. The card name must exactly match the value returned by Name (case-sensitive).

Example usage: 

slots.InstallCard("Disk II Controller", SlotNumber.Slot6);
slots.InstallCard("Super Serial Card", SlotNumber.Slot2);

If a card is already installed in the specified slot, it is replaced by the new card.

Exceptions

InvalidOperationException

Thrown if the card factory cannot create a card with the specified name.

See Also
InstallCard(int, SlotNumber)
RemoveCard(SlotNumber)

IsEmpty(SlotNumber)

Determines whether the specified slot is empty (contains a NullCard).

bool IsEmpty(SlotNumber slot)

Parameters

slot SlotNumber

The slot number (1-7) to check.

Returns

bool

true if the slot contains a GetNullCard() (empty slot); false if the slot contains an actual peripheral card.

Remarks

This is a convenience method that checks whether the card in the specified slot has an Id of 0 (the NullCard identifier). It provides a more readable alternative to manually checking the card ID.

Usage Examples: 

// Clear alternative to checking card ID
if (slots.IsEmpty(SlotNumber.Slot6))
{
    slots.InstallCard("Disk II Controller", SlotNumber.Slot6);
}

// List all installed cards
for (int i = 1; i <= 7; i++)
{
    var slot = (SlotNumber)(i - 1);
    if (!slots.IsEmpty(slot))
    {
        Console.WriteLine($"Slot {i}: {slots.GetCardIn(slot).Name}");
    }
}

This method is equivalent to: 

bool IsEmpty(SlotNumber slot) => GetCardIn(slot).Id == 0;
See Also
GetCardIn(SlotNumber)
RemoveCard(SlotNumber)

RemoveCard(SlotNumber)

Removes the card from the specified slot, replacing it with an empty slot (NullCard).

void RemoveCard(SlotNumber slot)

Parameters

slot SlotNumber

The slot number (1-7) to clear.

Remarks

This method replaces the card in the specified slot with a GetNullCard() instance. The NullCard returns null for all read operations and no-ops for all writes, causing the slot to behave as if it's empty (returning floating bus values for reads).

Any state maintained by the removed card is lost. If the removed card was the active extended ROM slot (see BankSelect), the extended ROM remains active but will return floating bus values since NullCard returns null.

Note: This method cannot fail due to the card being "in use" since the emulator doesn't maintain references to installed cards outside the slots array.

Exceptions

InvalidOperationException

Thrown if the card factory cannot create a NullCard replacement.

See Also
InstallCard(int, SlotNumber)
InstallCard(string, SlotNumber)

See Also

IMemory
IConfigurable
ICard
ICardFactory
